title: Real-Time Chat Application
statement:
Design a real-time chat application using WebSockets. Your application should allow users to connect to a chat room, send messages, and receive messages from other users in real-time.

Implement the following endpoints:

- `GET /api/rooms`: Returns a list of available chat rooms.
- `GET /api/rooms/:roomId`: Returns the details of the specified chat room.
- `POST /api/rooms/:roomId/messages`: Sends a new message to the specified chat room.

Use the following constraints:

- Must use Socket.IO as the WebSocket library.
- Must use a single WebSocket connection to handle all messages.
- Must use a data store (in-memory array) to store chat room names and messages.
- Must handle errors and exceptions properly.

Note: This is a hard difficulty question. You will need to demonstrate a good understanding of WebSockets, Socket.IO, and error handling.

**Example Use Cases**

- A user connects to the chat application and sends a message to a chat room.
- Another user in the same chat room receives the message in real-time.

**Constraints**

- Must handle disconnections and reconnections properly.
- Must use authentication (username and password) to ensure secure access to chat rooms.

**Data Store**

- Chat rooms: an array of objects with the following properties:
  - `id`: the room ID
  - `name`: the room name
  - `users`: an array of connected user IDs
- Messages: an array of objects with the following properties:
  - `id`: the message ID
  - `roomId`: the room ID
  - `text`: the message text
  - `timestamp`: the message timestamp
  - `username`: the username of the sender

**Visible Test Cases**

- `GET /api/rooms`: Returns a list of available chat rooms. Expected status code: 200.
- `GET /api/rooms/1`: Returns the details of chat room 1. Expected status code: 200.
- `POST /api/rooms/1/messages`: Sends a new message to chat room 1. Expected status code: 201.

**Hidden Test Cases**

- `GET /api/rooms/999`: Returns a 404 error as the room does not exist. Expected status code: 404.
- `POST /api/rooms/999/messages`: Returns a 404 error as the room does not exist. Expected status code: 404.
- `POST /api/rooms/1/messages`: Returns a 500 error as the message is invalid. Expected status code: 500.
constraints: Must use Socket.IO as the WebSocket library.
Must handle disconnections and reconnections properly.
Must use authentication (username and password) to ensure secure access to chat rooms.

Returns the details of the specified chat room.
Sends a new message to the specified chat room.
